briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware firm Synapse, raising serious questions about consumer protections in embedded finance.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing the boundaries of traditional credit card products with increasingly niche rewards and novel structures.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing interest in leveraging financial data for credit and payments services.

- **[Finance ministers and bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c2ev24yx4rmo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Global finance leaders are sounding the alarm over the Mythos AI model's unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cybersecurity vulnerabilities in financial systems.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Inside Wealthsimple's "simple" effort to bring Canadian talent home](https://betakit.com/inside-wealthsimples-simple-effort-to-bring-canadian-talent-home/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech leader Wealthsimple is actively recruiting Canadian tech talent back from abroad as it looks to strengthen its domestic workforce amid a competitive global market.

- **[Why the future of cross-border payments will be built through partnership](https://betakit.com/why-the-future-of-cross-border-payments-will-be-built-through-partnership/)** — *BetaKit* — A new analysis argues that collaboration between fintechs, banks, and payment networks will be the key driver of innovation in cross-border payments.

- **[Carney's pitch to unlock trillions in global investment](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/armstrong-invest-in-canada-summit-carney-9.7166810?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Prime Minister Carney is making a major push to attract global investment into Canada, with implications for the country's financial services and tech sectors.

- **[Canada opens applications to build a public AI supercomputer](https://betakit.com/canada-opens-applications-to-build-a-public-ai-supercomputer/)** — *BetaKit* — The Canadian government has opened applications for organizations to build a publicly accessible AI supercomputer, a move that could accelerate AI-driven fintech development across the country.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Evergreen relevance)* Canada's early push to replace screen scraping with secure open banking APIs set the stage for the country's ongoing financial data reform journey.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition, with lessons directly applicable to Canada's evolving open banking rollout and Atlantic Canadian credit unions navigating new market dynamics.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A thought-provoking collection of cautionary fintech stories that serves as a useful reminder for ecosystem builders in Atlantic Canada about the importance of consumer-centred, inclusive fintech design.
